frameworks.name: perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: training-induced plasticity for complex object discrimination, contrasted with simple-feature learning

frameworks.name: face-selective high-level visual processing
frameworks.one_line: size-, location-, and identity-invariant face-view representation refined by training

core_question: How does training to discriminate in-depth face-view orientation reshape behavior and neural representations of complex objects?
key_assumptions: face views can be discriminated along an in-depth orientation axis
perceptual learning operates differently for complex objects than for simple features
N170 and induced gamma index face-specific processing stages
manipulations: eight daily training sessions around a 30 deg face view
transfer tests across size, visual field, and face identity
transfer tests across partial face images, inversion, and a paperclip object
trained vs untrained face view (EEG and fMRI)
key_findings: training improved sensitivity to the trained face-view orientation, persisting up to six months
learning transferred across size, visual field, identity, and complementary partial-face images
very weak transfer to inverted faces or to a paperclip object
trained view yielded shorter N170 latency, reduced induced gamma, and more reliable left-FFA spatial pattern without BOLD amplitude change
interpretations: the visual system learned to compute face orientation from configural information more accurately
plastic changes occurred at a high-level stage representing size-, location-, and identity-invariant face views
